title: Lamb Kicked by Wok Fluffy Lamb Dim-Sum
description: A winey, fluffy, date-night Chinese dish featuring lamb mince, featuring a fermented kick.

ingredients.name: Lamb Mince
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use high-quality, grass-fed lamb for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Gao Li
ingredients.notes: A Chinese fermented soybean paste that adds depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce
ingredients.notes: Low-sodium soy sauce works best, as it won't overpower the other flavors.

ingredients.name: Sugar
ingredients.notes: Use superfine sugar for the best results.

ingredients.name: Cornstarch
ingredients.notes: Use superfine cornstarch for the best results.

instructions: Heat a tablespoon of vegetable oil in a wok over high heat until it starts to smoke.
Add the lamb mince to the wok and break it up with a spatula as it cooks, until it's browned and crispy, about 3 minutes.
Add the fermented Shaoxing wine, ginger, Gao Li, soy sauce, and sugar to the wok, and stir-fry until the liquid has almost completely evaporated, about 2 minutes.
Add the cornstarch to the wok and stir-fry for 1 minute, until the lamb is coated in the starch.
Transfer the lamb mixture to a bowl and set it aside to cool completely.
In a separate bowl, mix together the corn flour and a pinch of salt until well combined.
Add the scallions to the corn flour mixture and mix until they're evenly coated.
Using your hands, shape the scallion mixture into small dim-sum shapes, about 1 inch in diameter.
Heat a tablespoon of Wok Oil in a non-stick pan or wok over medium-high heat until it starts to smoke.
Add the lamb dim-sum to the pan and cook until they're golden brown on the bottom, about 2 minutes.
Flip the dim-sum over and cook until they're golden brown on the top, about 2 minutes.
Serve the lamb dim-sum hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
